channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Dontae Walker, former Mississippi State star running back, dead at 44
item.description: Former Mississippi State Bulldogs running back Dontae Walker has died, the school announced on Saturday. He was 44. He ran for more than 1,800 yards in his collegiate career.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles, Cowboys players brawl in tunnel after play, 3 ejected from game
item.description: Three players were ejected from the game after a brawl at the end of the Philadelphia Eagles' win over the Dallas Cowboys on Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saquon Barkley tops 2,000 yards rushing as Eagles beat Cowboys 41-7 to clinch NFC East
item.description: Saquon Barkley rushed for 167 yards to top 2,000 on the season, backup quarterback Kenny Pickett ran and threw for scores before departing with injured ribs, and the Philadelphia Eagles clinched the NFC East title by routing the Dallas Cowboys 41-7.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gelston, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mayfield throws 5 TD passes and Bucs keep playoff, NFC South hopes alive with 48-14 rout of Panthers
item.description: Baker Mayfield threw for 359 yards and five touchdowns to help the Tampa Bay Buccaneers keep their division and playoff hopes alive with a 48-14 rout of the Carolina Panthers.
item.dc:creator: Fred Goodall, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Saquon Barkley tops 2,000 yards rushing and moves within 100 of Dickerson's record
item.description: Saquon Barkley became the ninth running back in NFL history to top 2,000 yards rushing in a season, reaching the milestone with a 23-yard run in the fourth quarter against the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Feels like 1979: Nottingham Forest moves into 2nd place behind rampant Liverpool in Premier League
item.description: The Premier League table is starting to have a 1979 kind of feel to it — with Liverpool at the top of the standings and Nottingham Forest in second place as the closest challenger.
item.dc:creator: MATTIAS KAREN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Clearwater Beach hosts ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day
item.description: The ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day on Clearwater Beach kicked off at 11 a.m. with the festivities. But fans started lining their beach chairs up for a close view of the action much earlier.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Brew, WFLA, NBC

item.title: East Carolina-NC State fight mars end of Military Bowl, ref left bloodied
item.description: The Military Bowl between the East Carolina Pirates and the N.C. State Wolfpack ended with a massive brawl that led to the ejections of eight players.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James at 40: A milestone birthday arrives Monday for the NBA's all-time scoring leader
item.description: When LeBron James broke another NBA record earlier this month, the one for most regular-season minutes played in a career, his Los Angeles Lakers teammates handled the moment in typical locker room fashion. They made fun of him. “They told me I’m old as hell,” James quipped.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado football fans face a dramatic week across three teams
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Colorado football fans experienced a week filled with high-stakes action, featuring the Denver Broncos chasing a playoff berth and the Colorado Buffaloes and Colorado State Rams battling in bowl games.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Dallas' Naji Marshall gets 4-game suspension, Phoenix's Jusuf Nurkic is banned 3 games for fight
item.description: The NBA suspended Dallas Mavericks forward Naji Marshall for four games and Phoenix Suns center Jusuf Nurkic for three games for their roles in an on-court fight during Friday night's game. Dallas forward P.J. Washington was suspended one game. The suspensions handed down Saturday are without pay.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bengals keep playoff hopes alive with exhilarating overtime win over Broncos
item.description: The Cincinnati Bengals are holding onto their playoff hopes by a thread after a thrilling overtime victory over the Denver Broncos Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington State hires South Dakota State’s Jimmy Rogers as football coach
item.description: PULLMAN, Wash. (AP) — Washington State hired South Dakota State's Jimmy Rogers as its head football coach Saturday. Rogers replaces Jake Dickert, who left to become Wake Forest’s head coach Dec. 18.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia quarterback Carson Beck declares for NFL Draft after undergoing elbow surgery
item.description: Georgia Bulldogs quarterback Carson Beck, who just had elbow surgery, announced his decision to declare for the 2025 NFL Draft in a social media post Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Victor Wembanyama plays 1-on-1 chess with fans in New York
item.description: Victor Wembanyama went to a park in New York City and played 1-on-1 with fans on Saturday. He even lost a couple of games. Not in basketball, though. Wemby was playing chess. And this wasn't on a whim: He knows how to play and even brought his own chess set.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls blow 21 point lead in 141-133 loss to Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jalen Johnson had a career-high 30 points and the Atlanta Hawks rallied from a late 21-point deficit by scoring 50 points in the fourth quarter in a 141-133 win over the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Caitlin Clark honored as AP Female Athlete of the Year following her impact on women's sports
item.description: Caitlin Clark raised the profile of women's basketball to unprecedented levels in both the college ranks and the WNBA, and Tuesday she was named the AP Female Athlete of the Year for her impact on and off the court.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9
